content.text:
First, update the nginx.conf and docker-compose.yml to implement end-to-end TLS by configuring internal service-to-service encryption between the proxy and the backend/frontend upstreams, moving away from plaintext internal hops. Simultaneously, extend the AccountDeletionService to ensure an irreversible scrubbing of all PII; this must include the anonymization or deletion of fields in the MemberProfileEntity (such as phone_encrypted and phone_masked) and any other related tables to prevent residual data leakage post-deletion.

For the UX and Contract Alignment, synchronize the frontend discovery logic with the backend's canonical ranking by updating listings.ts and ListingDiscoveryPage to pass explicit SortMode and tag parameters to the server, rather than relying on local sorting. Enhance the AppealsPage to render reviewNotes and closure details for the submitting user, ensuring the appeal loop is transparent for all roles. Finally, update the ListingDetailPage to provide a complete view of benefits, specifically including free-shipping eligibility and explicit non-stacking explanations to match the checkout experience. Ensure all README documentation, particularly the migration inventory, is synchronized with the actual file count to maintain static verifiability.

1	# Demo Monorepo — Spring Boot + React + PostgreSQL
2	
3	A self-contained, offline-capable monorepo with a layered Spring Boot backend,
4	React frontend, PostgreSQL database, and TLS reverse proxy. Zero external
5	dependencies beyond Docker and Compose.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Quick Start
10	
11	```bash
12	docker compose up --build
13	```
14	
15	Requires Docker and Compose installed. On a clean volume, Flyway applies
16	21 migrations and seeds demo data. The frontend build expects network access
17	during `npm install`; subsequent starts use cached layers. Secrets are
18	generated at container startup via the entrypoint (no .env files required).
19	If `APP_JWT_SECRET` or `APP_ENCRYPTION_SECRET` are set in the environment,
20	those values take precedence over the auto-generated defaults.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Services and Ports
25	
26	| Service       | Container | Host Port    | URL                            |
27	|---------------|-----------|--------------|--------------------------------|
28	| PostgreSQL 16 | 5432      | internal     | not exposed to host            |
29	| Spring Boot   | 8080      | internal     | not exposed to host            |
30	| React (Vite)  | 3000      | internal     | not exposed to host            |

FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:resolve -B || true
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests -B

# Stage 2: Run
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
# Generate a self-signed PKCS12 keystore so the Spring Boot listener can
# serve HTTPS to the reverse proxy. This removes the plaintext upstream hop
# that the proxy used to make. The keystore lives inside the image (not in
# a mounted volume) and is regenerated on every build.
RUN apk add --no-cache openssl \
    && mkdir -p /app/certs \
    && openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 \
         -keyout /app/certs/backend.key \
         -out /app/certs/backend.crt \
         -subj "/C=US/ST=Local/L=Local/O=Demo/CN=backend" \
    && openssl pkcs12 -export \
         -in /app/certs/backend.crt \
         -inkey /app/certs/backend.key \
         -out /app/certs/backend.p12 \
         -name backend \
         -password pass:changeit \
    && rm -f /app/certs/backend.key /app/certs/backend.crt
COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8443
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
"}

## Summary

**End-to-end TLS** — proxy no longer makes plaintext hops. Backend listens on `8443` with a PKCS12 keystore baked into its image (`backend/Dockerfile:14-25`, `application.yml:25-37`). Frontend `serve` listens on `3443` with a self-signed cert (`frontend/Dockerfile`). `nginx.conf:8-16` points upstreams at those HTTPS ports and uses `proxy_pass https://…` with `proxy_ssl_server_name` and `proxy_ssl_verify off` for the self-signed internal certs. `docker-compose.yml` exposes the new ports and passes the SSL env vars. Test profile disables SSL in `application-test.yml:18-22`.

**Irreversible PII scrub** — `AccountDeletionService.processExpired` now calls `scrubUserData` which (1) nulls `emailLookupHash` on the user, (2) nulls `phoneEncrypted` + `phoneMasked` on the `MemberProfileEntity`, (3) clears address, cross-street, and description on every incident reported by the user, (4) deletes login_attempt rows keyed on the old username, and (5) overwrites `risk_event.details` JSON.

**Discovery / ranking sync** — `listings.ts` exports a `SortMode` type matching `ListingService.SortMode` and `searchListings` serializes `tags` as repeated params. `ListingDiscoveryPage` maps every UI sort key to a `SortMode`, sends `tags` to the server, drops the local sort/tag `useMemo`, and re-hits the server when sort/tags change.

**AppealsPage transparency** — a new "Review Outcome" column renders `reviewNotes`, `reviewedAt`, `resolvedAt`, and `reviewerId` for all roles; the mod-only actions column still shows only to moderators.

**ListingDetailPage benefits** — loads every active benefit (DISCOUNT, FREE_SHIPPING, PRIORITY_SUPPORT, EXCLUSIVE_ACCESS), renders a bulleted list with free-shipping eligibility, and spells out the non-stacking rules (one discount total, one item per exclusion group).
